content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). A tree is a connected undirected graph without cycles.

You have to assign an integer to each node; for each valid iii, let's denote the integer assigned to node iii by AiA_iAi‚Äč. This assignment must satisfy the following conditions:


For each valid iii, 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§2‚čÖ10181 \le A_i \le 2 \cdot 10^{18}1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖ1018.
For each simple path in the tree, let's denote the set of nodes in this path by SSS. Then, the product P=‚ąŹv‚ąąSAvP = \prod_{v \in S} A_vP=‚ąŹv‚ąąS‚ÄčAv‚Äč is divisible by ‚ą£S‚ą£|S|‚ą£S‚ą£.
constraints: This assignment satisfies all the conditions. For example, for the path 2‚Üí1‚Üí5‚Üí62 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 62‚Üí1‚Üí5‚Üí6, the product P=3‚čÖ2‚čÖ6‚čÖ5=180P = 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 6 \cdot 5 = 180P=3‚čÖ2‚čÖ6‚čÖ5=180 is divisible by 444 (the number of nodes in the path).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.

If there any multiple solutions, you may find any one of them. It can be proved a solution always exists under the given constraints.
